name: Ginkgo Bioworks Holdings, Inc.
business: Ginkgo Bioworks sells cell engineering tools and biological R&D services to customers across industries including pharmaceuticals, agriculture, industrial biotechnology, and government. It also provides biosecurity services, primarily to governments, for pathogen monitoring and threat detection. The company generates revenue from service fees and downstream value share arrangements.

exposures.sentence: Our customers in pharmaceutical and biotechnology use our services to develop new manufacturing methods for gene therapies, biologics, vaccines, and small molecule therapeutics
Our customers in agriculture use our services to improve the performance and manufacturability of existing agricultural biologics, develop revolutionary new products for crop nutrition
Our Autonomous Lab is a flexible wet lab built from our Reconfigurable Automation Cart (RAC) systems capable of large scale data generation
In August 2023, we entered into a strategic partnership with Google Cloud to develop and deploy AI tools for biology and biosecurity
Because biosecurity is a matter of national and global security, our primary biosecurity customers are governments.
Canopy, which helps our customers generate high value genomic data from strategically-positioned nodes (like airports and border checkpoints) via end-to-end biomonitoring programs

catalysts.event: One-time payment of $14.0 million to Google Cloud to be released from minimum annual commitment obligations
catalysts.sentence: The Company is required to make a one-time payment of $14.0 million to be released from its minimum annual commitment obligations under the original agreement in January 2026.

catalysts.event: Non-refundable payment of $5.0 million to Twist
catalysts.sentence: The Company paid $4.0 million in April 2025 and is obligated to non-refundable payments of $5.0 million on April 1, 2026 and $6.0 million on April 1, 2027, respectively.

catalysts.event: Non-refundable payment of $6.0 million to Twist
catalysts.sentence: The Company paid $4.0 million in April 2025 and is obligated to non-refundable payments of $5.0 million on April 1, 2026 and $6.0 million on April 1, 2027, respectively.

catalysts.event: Funding of $47.0 million surety bond for RAC automation equipment contract with U.S. Government National Laboratory
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, the Company will be required to fund a surety bond in the amount of $47.0 million to fulfill its obligations under a contract with a U.S. Government National Laboratory related to the sale of RAC automation equipment.

catalysts.event: Cash restricted until completion of RAC automation equipment contract obligations
catalysts.sentence: Currently the Company expects the cash to be restricted until 2029.
